label: Multilayer perceptrons

node.label: Multilayer perceptrons
node.definition: A multilayer perceptron stacks affine transformations and nonlinear activations to learn general nonlinear mappings over fixed-size feature vectors.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Multilayer perceptrons is to connect Affine transforms & dense layers, Nonlinear activations to downstream capabilities including Neural fields & radiance-field models.
examples: substrate for Neural fields & radiance-field models
combined with Affine transforms & dense layers
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Affine transforms & dense layers, Nonlinear activations.

dependencies.rationale: Multilayer perceptrons is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
Multilayer perceptrons is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Nonlinear activations.
Multilayer perceptrons is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Residual, skip & highway connections.
Multilayer perceptrons follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
